name: model-bakery

metadata.name: model-bakery
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: Apache Software License
metadata.summary: Smart object creation facility for Django.

documentation:
# Model Bakery: Smart fixtures for better tests

[![Build](https://img.shields.io/github/actions/workflow/status/model-bakers/model_bakery/ci.yml?branch=main)](https://github.com/model-bakers/model_bakery/actions?workflow=Tests)
[![Coverage](https://img.shields.io/badge/Coverage-97%25-success)](https://github.com/model-bakers/model_bakery/actions?workflow=Tests)
[![Latest PyPI version](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/model_bakery.svg)](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/model_bakery/)
[![Documentation Status](https://readthedocs.org/projects/model-bakery/badge/?version=latest)](https://model-bakery.readthedocs.io/en/latest/?badge=latest)

*Model Bakery* offers you a smart way to create fixtures for testing in
Django.
With a simple and powerful API you can create many objects with a single
line of code.

Model Bakery is a rename of the legacy [Model Mommy project](https://pypi.org/project/model_mommy/).

## Install

```bash
pip install model-bakery
```

## Usage and Info

### Basic usage

```python

# models.py

from django.db import models

class Customer(models.Model):
    enjoy_jards_macale = models.BooleanField()
    name = models.CharField(max_length=30)
    email = models.EmailField()
    age = models.IntegerField()
    bio = models.TextField()
    days_since_last_login = models.BigIntegerField()
    birthday = models.DateField()
    last_shopping = models.DateTimeField()

# test_models.py

from django.test import TestCase
from model_bakery import baker
from pprint import pprint

class TestCustomerModel(TestCase):
    def setUp(self):
        self.customer = baker.make('shop.Customer')
        pprint(self.customer.__dict__)

"""
{'_state': <django.db.models.base.ModelState object at 0x1129a3240>,
 'age': 3841,
 'bio': '',
 'birthday': datetime.date(2019, 12, 3),
 'enjoy_jards_macale': True,
 'id': 1,
 'last_shopping': datetime.datetime(2019, 12, 3, 21, 42, 34, 77019),
 'name': 'qiayYnESvqcYLLBzxpFOcGBIfnQEPx',
 'days_since_last_login': 6016}
"""

```

Check out [documentation](<http://model-bakery.readthedocs.org/>) for more complete examples.

## Contributing

Detailed info [here](https://github.com/model-bakers/model_bakery/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md).

functions.name: model_bakery.utils.import_from_str
documentation:
Import an object defined as import if it is an string.

If `import_string` follows the format `path.to.module.object_name`,
this method imports it; else it just return the object.

functions.name: model_bakery.utils.get_calling_module
documentation:
Get the module some number of stack frames back from the current one.

Make sure to account for the number of frames between the "calling" code
and the one that calls this function.

Args:
    levels_back (int): Number of stack frames back from the current

Returns:
    (ModuleType): the module from which the code was called

functions.name: model_bakery.utils.seq
documentation:
Generate a sequence of values based on a running count.

This function can be used to generate sequences of `int`, `float`,
`datetime`, `date`, `time`, or `str`: whatever the `type` is of the
provided `value`.

Args:
    value (object): the value at which to begin generation (this will
        be ignored for types `datetime`, `date`, and `time`)
    increment_by (`int` or `float`, optional): the amount by which to
        increment for each generated value (defaults to `1`)
    start (`int` or `float`, optional): the value at which the sequence
        will begin to add to `value` (if `value` is a `str`, `start` will
        be appended to it)
    suffix (`str`, optional): for `str` `value` sequences, this will be
        appended to the end of each generated value (after the counting
        value is appended)

Returns:
    object: generated values for sequential data

functions.name: model_bakery.recipe._load_recipe_from_calling_module
documentation:
Load `Recipe` from the string attribute given from the calling module.

Args:
    recipe (str): the name of the recipe attribute within the module from
        which it should be loaded

Returns:
    (Recipe): recipe resolved from calling module

functions.name: model_bakery.recipe.foreign_key
documentation:
Return a `RecipeForeignKey`.

Return the callable, so that the associated `_model` will not be created
during the recipe definition.

This resolves recipes supplied as strings from other module paths or from
the calling code's module.

classes.name: model_bakery.recipe.RecipeForeignKey
documentation: A `Recipe` to use for making ManyToOne and OneToOne related objects.

methods.name: model_bakery.recipe.related.make
documentation: Persist objects to m2m relation.

modules.name: model_bakery.timezone
documentation: Utility functions to manage timezone code.

functions.name: model_bakery.timezone.tz_aware
documentation: Return an UTC-aware datetime in case of USE_TZ=True.

modules.name: model_bakery.random_gen
documentation:
Generators are callables that return a value used to populate a field.

If this callable has a `required` attribute (a list, mostly), for each
item in the list, if the item is a string, the field attribute with the
same name will be fetched from the field and used as argument for the
generator. If it is a callable (which will receive `field` as first
argument), it should return a list in the format (key, value) where key
is the argument name for generator and value is the value for that
argument.

functions.name: model_bakery.random_gen.gen_from_list
documentation:
Make sure all values of the field are generated from a list.

Examples:
    Here how to use it.

    >>> from baker import Baker
    >>> class ExperienceBaker(Baker):
    >>>     attr_mapping = {'some_field': gen_from_list(['A', 'B', 'C'])}

documentation: -- DEFAULT GENERATORS --

functions.name: model_bakery.generators.get_type_mapping
documentation: Add GIS fields

variables.name: model_bakery.generators.default_mapping
documentation: type: Dict[Type, Callable]

functions.name: model_bakery.baker.make
documentation:
Create a persisted instance from a given model its associated models.

Baker fills the fields with random values, or you can specify which
fields you want to define its values by yourself.

functions.name: model_bakery.baker.prepare
documentation:
Create but do not persist an instance from a given model.

Baker fills the fields with random values, or you can specify which
fields you want to define its values by yourself.

functions.name: model_bakery.baker._custom_baker_class
documentation:
Return the specified custom baker class.

Returns:
    object: The custom class is specified by BAKER_CUSTOM_CLASS in Django's
    settings, or None if no custom class is defined.

functions.name: model_bakery.baker.get_required_values
documentation:
Get required values for a generator from the field.

If required value is a function, calls it with field as argument. If
required value is a string, simply fetch the value from the field
and return.

functions.name: model_bakery.baker._save_related_objs
documentation: Recursively save all related foreign keys for each entry.

functions.name: model_bakery.baker.bulk_create
documentation:
Bulk create entries and all related FKs as well.

Important: there's no way to avoid save calls since Django does
not return the created objects after a bulk_insert call.

classes.name: model_bakery.baker.ModelFinder

methods.name: model_bakery.baker.ModelFinder.get_model
documentation:
Get a model.

Args:
name (str): A name on the form 'applabel.modelname' or 'modelname'

Returns:
    object: a model class

methods.name: model_bakery.baker.ModelFinder.get_model_by_name
documentation:
Get a model by name.

If a model with that name exists in more than one app, raises
AmbiguousModelName.

methods.name: model_bakery.baker.ModelFinder._populate
documentation: Cache models for faster self._get_model.
Encapsulates all the logic for finding a model to Baker.

methods.name: model_bakery.baker.Baker.create
documentation: Create the baker class defined by the `BAKER_CUSTOM_CLASS` setting.

methods.name: model_bakery.baker.Baker.make
documentation: Create and persist an instance of the model associated with Baker instance.

methods.name: model_bakery.baker.Baker.prepare
documentation: Create, but do not persist, an instance of the associated model.

methods.name: model_bakery.baker.Baker._handle_generic_foreign_keys
documentation: Set content type and object id for GenericForeignKey fields.

methods.name: model_bakery.baker.Baker.generate_value
documentation:
Call the associated generator with a field passing all required args.

Generator Resolution Precedence Order:
-- `field.default` - model field default value, unless explicitly overwritten during baking
-- `field.db_default` - model field db default value, unless explicitly overwritten
-- `attr_mapping` - mapping per attribute name
-- `choices` -- mapping from available field choices
-- `type_mapping` - mapping from user defined type associated generators
-- `default_mapping` - mapping from pre-defined type associated
   generators

`attr_mapping` and `type_mapping` can be defined easily overwriting the
model.
